meaning: adj.粒状的,木纹状的,多粒的
examples: The film is shot in grainy black and white.
Was it too full of content? Are the images grainy?
ANALYSIS: Sensing Salt on the Sea — No More Grainy pictures.
Camera-phones were still a novelty that captured grainy, low-res images, and digital cameras didn't fit in jeans pockets.
The magazine prints these found submissions using grainy, black-and-white photocopies seemingly taped together on the page.
